body:
formal-ai issue link-assistant/formal-ai#96 is integrating link-calculator as its delegated calculator backend and found one math-shaped expression that must stay on formal-ai's local fallback for now.

Reproduction with link-calculator v0.16.0:

```text
100 - 25 % 7
```

Actual result:

```text
Result: 99.75
Links notation: (100 - (25 / 100))
```

The trailing `7` is not represented in the Links Notation or the steps. For calculator users, `%` can mean postfix percent in expressions like `8% of 0`, but it is also commonly used as binary remainder/modulo in programming/math-like prompts such as `100 - 25 % 7`.

Expected behavior:

- Either parse binary modulo/remainder for math-like infix usage, producing `96` for `100 - 25 % 7`; or
- reject the expression instead of accepting a prefix and dropping the trailing operand.

A related prompt also shows why callers need strict full-input handling:

```text
2 + 2 please
```

Actual result is `4 please`, because `please` is treated as a custom unit. Natural-language callers can strip wrappers before delegation, but the calculator should still avoid silently accepting unintended trailing text when possible.

formal-ai workaround: keep English word-operator arithmetic and binary `%` remainder expressions on its local fallback until this behavior is supported upstream.

comments.body:
We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

title: Support binary modulo and reject trailing tokens after postfix percent
